# Starter Kits

Sources used: Context7 `/websites/laravel_13_x` results for Laravel 13.x starter kits, authentication quickstart, and frontend starter kits.

## Creating With Starter Kit Selection
The starter kits docs show creating an application with the Laravel installer:

```shell
laravel new my-app
```

The docs state the installer prompts for starter kit selection.

## Authentication Scaffolding
The installation docs state Laravel starter kits offer backend and frontend authentication scaffolding to provide a head start when developing an application.

The authentication docs state starter kits scaffold an entire authentication system, including necessary controllers, routes, and views.

The authentication docs also state that by installing a starter kit in a fresh application and running database migrations, developers can immediately access registration and login functionality.

The docs describe starter kits as an educational resource for developers who want to build a custom authentication flow.

## Frontend Starter Kit Surface
The frontend docs state Laravel provides application starter kits with pre-configured setups for Inertia, React, Svelte, or Vue.

The frontend docs state these starter kits scaffold backend and frontend authentication flows and integrate Tailwind CSS and Vite.

The frontend docs describe these starter kits as the recommended way to jump-start development and as a production-ready foundation for a project.

## Topics Requiring Fresh Lookup
Run a new Context7 query before naming additional starter kits, using exact starter kit flags, describing generated files beyond controllers/routes/views, choosing between frontend stacks, customizing authentication, adding social login, using API token authentication, or describing package internals.
